001package guiUserLogin;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.Scene;
005import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
006import javafx.scene.control.Button;
007import javafx.scene.control.Label;
008import javafx.scene.control.PasswordField;
009import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
010import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
011import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
012import javafx.scene.text.Font;
013import javafx.stage.Stage;
014
015
016/*******
017 * <p> Title: GUIStartupPage Class. </p>
018 * 
019 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based System Startup Page for user login.</p>
020 * 
021 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
022 * 
023 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
024 * 
025 * @version 1.00                2025-04-20 Initial version
026 *  
027 */
028
029public class ViewUserLogin {
030
031        /*-********************************************************************************************
032
033        Attributes
034
035         *********************************************************************************************/
036
037        // These are the application values required by the user interface
038
039        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
040        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
041
042        private static Label label_ApplicationTitle = new Label("Foundation Application Startup Page");
043
044        // This set is for all subsequent starts of the system
045        private static Label label_OperationalStartTitle = new Label("User Login");
046        private static Label label_LogInInsrtuctions = new Label("Enter your user name and password and "+      
047                        "then click on the LogIn button");
048        protected static Alert alertUsernamePasswordError = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
049
050
051        //      private User user;
052        protected static TextField text_Username = new TextField();
053        protected static PasswordField text_Password = new PasswordField();
054        private static Button button_Login = new Button("Log In");      
055
056        // Invitation code for new users
057        private static Label label_InvitationInstructions = new Label("New user? Enter invitation code to sign up");
058        protected static TextField text_InvitationCode = new TextField();
059        private static Button button_UseInvitationCode = new Button("Use Invitation Code");
060        protected static Alert alertInvitationError = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
061        
062        // Create new account without invitation
063        private static Button button_CreateNewAccount = new Button("Create New Account");
064
065        private static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
066
067        private static Stage theStage;  
068        private static Pane theRootPane;
069        public static Scene theUserLoginScene = null;   
070
071
072        private static ViewUserLogin theView = null;    //      private static guiUserLogin.ControllerUserLogin theController;
073
074
075        /*-********************************************************************************************
076
077        Constructor
078
079         *********************************************************************************************/
080
081        public static void displayUserLogin(Stage ps) {
082                
083                // Establish the references to the GUI. There is no current user yet.
084                theStage = ps;
085                
086                // If not yet established, populate the static aspects of the GUI
087                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewUserLogin();
088                
089                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
090                // state of the system.         
091                text_Username.setText("");              // Reset the username and password from the last use
092                text_Password.setText("");
093                text_InvitationCode.setText("");
094
095                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
096                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: User Login Page");          
097                theStage.setScene(theUserLoginScene);
098                theStage.show();
099        }
100
101        /**********
102         * <p> Method: ViewUserLoginPage() </p>
103         * 
104         * <p> Description: This method is called when the application first starts. It must handle
105         * two cases: 1) when no user has been established and 2) when one or more users have been 
106         * established.
107         * 
108         * If there are no users in the database, this means that the person starting the system must
109         * be an administrator, so a special GUI is provided to allow this Admin to set a username and
110         * password.
111         * 
112         * If there is at least one user, then a display is shown for existing users to login.</p>
113         * 
114         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
115         * 
116         * @param theRoot specifies the JavaFX Pane to be used for this GUI and it's methods
117         * 
118         * @param db specifies the Database to be used by this GUI and it's methods
119         * 
120         */
121        private ViewUserLogin() {
122
123                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
124                theRootPane = new Pane();
125                theUserLoginScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
126                
127                // Populate the window with the title and other common widgets and set their static state
128                setupLabelUI(label_ApplicationTitle, "Arial", 32, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 10);
129
130                setupLabelUI(label_OperationalStartTitle, "Arial", 24, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 60);
131
132
133                // Existing user log in portion of the page
134
135                setupLabelUI(label_LogInInsrtuctions, "Arial", 18, width, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 120);
136
137                // Establish the text input operand field for the username
138                setupTextUI(text_Username, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 160, true);
139                text_Username.setPromptText("Enter Username");
140
141                // Establish the text input operand field for the password
142                setupTextUI(text_Password, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 210, true);
143                text_Password.setPromptText("Enter Password");
144
145                // Set up the Log In button
146                setupButtonUI(button_Login, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 475, 180);
147                button_Login.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerUserLogin.doLogin(theStage); });
148
149                alertUsernamePasswordError.setTitle("Invalid username/password!");
150                alertUsernamePasswordError.setHeaderText(null);
151
152                // Invitation code section
153                setupLabelUI(label_InvitationInstructions, "Arial", 16, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 260);
154                setupTextUI(text_InvitationCode, "Arial", 18, 220, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 300, true);
155                text_InvitationCode.setPromptText("Enter invitation code");
156                setupButtonUI(button_UseInvitationCode, "Dialog", 18, 220, Pos.CENTER, 320, 300);
157                button_UseInvitationCode.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerUserLogin.doInvitationCode(theStage); });
158                alertInvitationError.setTitle("Invalid Invitation Code");
159                alertInvitationError.setHeaderText(null);
160                
161                // Create new account button
162                setupButtonUI(button_CreateNewAccount, "Dialog", 18, 220, Pos.CENTER, 320, 350);
163                button_CreateNewAccount.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerUserLogin.doCreateNewAccount(theStage); });
164
165                // Set up the Quit button  
166                setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 520);
167                button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerUserLogin.performQuit(); });
168
169                theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
170                                label_ApplicationTitle, 
171                                label_OperationalStartTitle,
172                                label_LogInInsrtuctions, text_Username,
173                                button_Login, text_Password,
174                                label_InvitationInstructions, text_InvitationCode,
175                                button_UseInvitationCode,
176                                button_CreateNewAccount,
177                                button_Quit);
178        }
179
180
181        /*-********************************************************************************************
182
183        Helper methods to reduce code length
184
185         *********************************************************************************************/
186
187        /**********
188         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
189         */
190
191        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
192                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
193                l.setMinWidth(w);
194                l.setAlignment(p);
195                l.setLayoutX(x);
196                l.setLayoutY(y);                
197        }
198
199
200        /**********
201         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
202         * 
203         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
204         * @param ff    The font to be used
205         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
206         * @param w             The width of the Button
207         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
208         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
209         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
210         */
211        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
212                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
213                b.setMinWidth(w);
214                b.setAlignment(p);
215                b.setLayoutX(x);
216                b.setLayoutY(y);                
217        }
218
219        /**********
220         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text field
221         */
222        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e){
223                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
224                t.setMinWidth(w);
225                t.setMaxWidth(w);
226                t.setAlignment(p);
227                t.setLayoutX(x);
228                t.setLayoutY(y);                
229                t.setEditable(e);
230        }               
231}
